1190	/- Planner strategy:
1191	Goal: `pushPullObj F Y_p ≅ ∏_σ pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)))` in `X.Modules`.
1192	where `Y_p = (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (op (mk p))`.
1193	
1194	This is THE new-infra leaf. The key observation is that, although the opens `U_σ ⊆ X` OVERLAP
1195	inside `X`, they are DISJOINT as components of the coproduct scheme `Y_p = ∐_σ U_σ`.
1196	
1197	Route:
1198	(a) TRANSPORT via `cechBackbone_left_sigma`: by the iso from Stub 1, we may work with the
1199	    coproduct `∐_σ Over.mk j_σ` instead of `Y_p`.
1200	
1201	(b) BUILD comparison map:
1202	    `pushPullObj F Y_p ⟶ ∏_σ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)`
1203	    from the projections `pushPullMap F (ι_σ) : pushPullObj F Y_p ⟶ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)`
1204	    induced by the coproduct inclusions `ι_σ : Over.mk j_σ ⟶ Y_p` (going backwards via
1205	    the pushPullFunctor, which is contravariant on `Over X`).
1206	
1207	(c) CHECK iso via `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf`:
1208	    The forgetful functor `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf = SheafOfModules.forget ⋙
1209	    PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf ...` is faithful, reflects isos, and preserves limits
1210	    (`Sheaf.lean:75–78`). So it suffices to verify the comparison is an iso at the
1211	    `Ab`-presheaf level.
1212	
1213	(d) PRESHEAF-LEVEL ISO: on `Ab`-presheaves, this is the indexed disjoint-union decomposition.
1214	    Since the components of `∐_σ U_σ` are disjoint in the coproduct topology:
1215	    * Iterate the binary `TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint` (Lean name: same) over the
1216	      finite index set `{σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀}`.
1217	    * Or use `Scheme.coprodPresheafObjIso` (sections over a binary coproduct scheme = product)
1218	      as the binary building block and iterate.
1219	    The result: for any open `W` in `Y_p`, `(q_p^* F).val.obj (op W) ≅ ∏_σ (j_σ^* F).val.obj (op (W_σ))`
1220	    where `W_σ = (ι_σ)⁻¹W` is the trace on the σ-component.
1221	
1222	(e) TRANSPORT back through `toPresheaf` to get the iso in `X.Modules`.
1223	
1224	Key Mathlib anchors:
1225	- `TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint` (Topology/Sheaves/SheafCondition/PairwiseIntersections.lean)
1226	- `Scheme.coprodPresheafObjIso` (AlgebraicGeometry/Limits.lean)
1227	- `SheafOfModules.forget` faithfulness and iso-reflection (`Sheaf.lean:75–78`)
1228	
1229	Difficulty: HARD (genuine new sheaf infra — the single new-infra leaf of the chain). -/
1230	-- Instance synthesis for the three chained `pushPullObjCongr`/`pushPull_coprod_prod` isos over the
1231	-- Čech backbone (`HasProduct`/`HasCoproduct` on the `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀`-indexed slice families)
1232	-- exceeds the default `synthInstance` budget; bump it for this assembly.
1233	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1234	noncomputable def pushPull_sigma_iso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
1235	    (F : X.Modules) (p : ℕ) :
1236	    pushPullObj F ((coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p))) ≅
1237	    ∏ᶜ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
1238	      pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))) :=
1239	  -- The backbone `Y_p` is the coproduct `∐_σ Over.mk j_σ` (Stub 1); transport the push–pull
1240	  -- object across that iso and the `overSigmaDescIso` identification of `∐` with the descent
1241	  -- object, then apply the finite coproduct→product decomposition `pushPull_coprod_prod`.
1242	  let legs : (Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) → Over X :=
1243	    fun σ => Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))
1244	  pushPullObjCongr F (cechBackbone_left_sigma 𝒰 p) ≪≫
1245	    pushPullObjCongr F (overSigmaDescIso (fun σ => (legs σ).hom)) ≪≫
1246	    pushPull_coprod_prod F legs
1247	
1248	/-- **The `σ`-leg of the Stub-2 product decomposition** (seam (a) of the
1249	`coreIso_comm_leg` effort-break): projecting `pushPull_sigma_iso` onto the `σ`-component
1250	gives the push–pull map of the canonical over-inclusion of the `σ`-summand into the Čech
1251	backbone (the composite of the coproduct inclusion with the two backbone identifications,
1252	read backwards). -/
1253	lemma pushPull_sigma_iso_π (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules) (p : ℕ)
1254	    (σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) :
1255	    (pushPull_sigma_iso 𝒰 F p).hom ≫
1256	        Pi.π (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
1257	          pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)))) σ =
1258	      pushPullMap F (coprodOverIncl (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
1259	          Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))) σ ≫
1260	        (overSigmaDescIso (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
1261	          (Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))).hom)).inv ≫
1262	        (cechBackbone_left_sigma 𝒰 p).inv) := by
1263	  refine Eq.trans (Category.assoc _ _ _) ?_
1264	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg
1265	    (fun m => pushPullMap F (cechBackbone_left_sigma 𝒰 p).inv ≫ m)
1266	    (Category.assoc _ _ _)) ?_
1267	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg
1268	    (fun m => pushPullMap F (cechBackbone_left_sigma 𝒰 p).inv ≫
1269	      pushPullMap F (overSigmaDescIso (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
1270	        (Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))).hom)).inv ≫ m)
1271	    (coprodToProdMap_comp_π F
1272	      (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))) σ)) ?_
1273	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg
1274	    (fun m => pushPullMap F (cechBackbone_left_sigma 𝒰 p).inv ≫ m)
1275	    (pushPullMap_comp F _ _).symm) ?_
1276	  refine Eq.trans (pushPullMap_comp F _ _).symm ?_
1277	  exact congrArg (pushPullMap F) (Category.assoc _ _ _)
1278	
1279	/-! ## Stub 3 — Per-leg section identification -/
1280	
1281	/- Planner strategy:
1282	Goal: `Γ(V, pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)) ≅ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)` as `Ab` objects,
1283	where `j_σ = Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) : (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).toScheme ⟶ X`.
1284	
1285	Three off-the-shelf identifications, chained:
1286	
1287	(1) PUSHFORWARD SECTIONS = PREIMAGE SECTIONS (`pushforward_obj_obj`, `rfl`, Sheaf.lean:155):
1288	    `Γ(V, (j_σ)_* N) = Γ(j_σ⁻¹V, N)` for any `N : (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).toScheme.Modules`.
1289	    Apply to `N = (j_σ)^* F = Scheme.Modules.pullback j_σ |>.obj F`.
1290	
1291	(2) PULLBACK ALONG OPEN IMMERSION = RESTRICTION (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Sheaf.lean:371):
1292	    `(j_σ)^* F ≅ F.restrict j_σ` as `(coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).toScheme.Modules` objects.
1293	    This is already used in `QcohRestrictBasicOpen.lean:113–114,248`.
1294	
1295	(3) SECTIONS OF RESTRICTION = SECTIONS OF IMAGE-PREIMAGE (`restrict_obj`, `rfl`, Sheaf.lean:328):
1296	    `Γ(W, F.restrict j_σ) = Γ(j_σ ''ᵁ W, F)` for any `W` in the source scheme.
1297	    Applied to `W = j_σ⁻¹V`: `j_σ ''ᵁ (j_σ⁻¹V) = U_σ ∩ V` (since `j_σ` is an open
1298	    immersion: image-of-preimage = intersection with image = `U_σ ∩ V`).
1299

560	Project-local. -/
561	noncomputable def coverCechNerveOverAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
562	    SimplicialObject.Augmented (Over X) :=
563	  SimplicialObject.augment (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰) (Over.mk (𝟙 X))
564	    (Over.mkIdTerminal.from _)
565	    (fun _ _ _ => Over.mkIdTerminal.hom_ext _ _)
566	
567	/-- The cosimplicial `O_X`-module obtained by post-composing the over-category Čech
568	backbone `coverCechNerveOver` (read contravariantly, via `Functor.leftOp`) with the
569	push–pull functor `pushPullFunctor F = G`. This is the underlying cosimplicial object
570	of `CechNerve` (before adjoining the augmentation `F`). Project-local. -/
571	noncomputable def cechNerveCosimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
572	    CosimplicialObject X.Modules :=
573	  (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰 : SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ ⥤ Over X).rightOp ⋙ pushPullFunctor F
574	
575	/-- **Čech nerve of an affine open cover** (Stacks, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech
576	cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves).
577	
578	For a scheme `X`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰 : X = ⋃ Uᵢ` and a quasi-coherent
579	sheaf `F`, the *Čech nerve* is the augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules whose
580	object in simplicial degree `p` is the product, over the `(p+1)`-tuples `(i₀,…,i_p)` of
581	indices, of the direct images `(j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})` of the restriction of
582	`F` to the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{i₀…i_p} = U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{i_p}` along the open
583	immersion `j_{i₀…i_p} : U_{i₀…i_p} ↪ X`. Faces are the restriction maps that omit one
584	index, degeneracies repeat one index, and the augmentation in degree `-1` is `F` itself
585	on all of `X`. When `X` is separated each intersection `U_{i₀…i_p}` is affine.
586	
587	This is now *constructed*, not postulated: the geometric backbone `coverCechNerveOverAug`
588	(the augmented Čech nerve of the cover as an augmented simplicial object in `Over X`,
589	unconditional because `Scheme` has all finite limits) is read contravariantly
590	(`SimplicialObject.Augmented.rightOp`) and whiskered by the push–pull functor
591	`pushPullFunctor F = G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. The whiskering
592	transports both the cosimplicial structure and the augmentation, so the augmentation
593	point is `G` applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`, namely
594	`(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F ≅ F`. The functor laws of `G` (`pushPullMap_id`, `pushPullMap_comp`)
595	are what make this assembly legitimate.
596	
597	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
598	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
599	noncomputable def CechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
600	    CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules :=
601	  (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj (Over X)ᵒᵖ X.Modules (pushPullFunctor F)).obj
602	    (coverCechNerveOverAug 𝒰).rightOp
603	
604	/-- **Relative {\v C}ech complex from a cosimplicial nerve** (coherence-free
605	plumbing). Given `f : X ⟶ S` and an augmented cosimplicial object `N` of
606	`O_X`-modules, produce the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` by:
607	forgetting the augmentation (`CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop`), pushing the
608	cosimplicial object forward along `f` (`CosimplicialObject.whiskering` applied to
609	`Scheme.Modules.pushforward f`), and taking the alternating coface-map cochain
610	complex (`alternatingCofaceMapComplex`). This is the entire passage `CechNerve ↦
611	CechComplex`, and it uses no `pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` coherence — only
612	the (pre)additivity of `S.Modules`. Project-local. -/
613	noncomputable def relativeCechComplexOfNerve (f : X ⟶ S)
614	    (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules) : CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
615	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex S.Modules).obj
616	    (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering X.Modules S.Modules).obj
617	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)).obj (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N))
618	
619	/-- **Relative Čech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf** (Stacks, Cohomology of
620	Schemes, `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`).
621	
622	For `f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X` (with all intersections
623	affine, e.g. `X` separated) and a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`, the *relative Čech
624	complex* `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the cochain complex of `O_S`-modules with degree-`p` term
625	```
626	  Čᵖ(𝒰, F) = ∏_{(i₀,…,i_p)} (f|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}}),
627	```
628	and differential the alternating sum of the restriction maps
629	`(d s)_{i₀…i_{p+1}} = Σⱼ (-1)ʲ s_{i₀…î_j…i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i₀…i_{p+1}}}`. Over an
630	affine `U = Spec A` with `F|_U = M~` and a standard cover by the `D(fᵢ)`, this is
631	the complex of localisations `∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}f_{i₁}} → ⋯`. Each term
632	is quasi-coherent because the intersections are affine and the pushforward of a
633	quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated morphism is
634	quasi-coherent.
635	
636	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
637	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
638	noncomputable def CechComplex (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
639	    CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
640	  -- Construction (Stacks): apply the relative pushforward `f_*` over each finite
641	  -- intersection to the Čech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F`, then take the alternating-sum
642	  -- Čech differential. This is exactly the coherence-free plumbing
643	  -- `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`, so `CechComplex` is genuinely *defined* in terms
644	  -- of the nerve: an axiom-clean `CechNerve` immediately yields an axiom-clean
645	  -- `CechComplex`. The only remaining hole is `CechNerve` itself.
646	  relativeCechComplexOfNerve f (CechNerve 𝒰 F)
647	
648	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the augmented Čech complex on `X`
649	
650	The lemma `cechAugmented_exact` (`lem:cech_augmented_resolution`) asserts that the
651	augmented {\v C}ech complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` of a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`
652	over an affine open cover `𝒰` is **exact** in `QCoh(X)` — i.e. the {\v C}ech nerve
653	is a resolution of `F`. This is the *un-pushed* (over `X`, not relative to `f`)
654	counterpart of `CechComplex`/`relativeCechComplexOfNerve`. We record here the
655	structural ingredients that the exactness statement is phrased against: the
656	un-augmented {\v C}ech complex on `X` (`cechComplexOnX`), obtained from the {\v C}ech
657	nerve exactly as `relativeCechComplexOfNerve` does but with the *identity*
658	pushforward — i.e. directly, with no `f_*`. -/
659	
660	/-- **The (un-augmented) {\v C}ech cochain complex of `F` on `X`.** This is the
661	alternating-coface-map complex `C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` of the underlying cosimplicial object
662	of the {\v C}ech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F` (its augmentation `F` is dropped). It is the
663	`f = 𝟙`/un-pushed analogue of `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`: the same coherence-free
664	plumbing without the relative pushforward `f_*`, so it is the complex whose
665	exactness (after re-adjoining the augmentation `F → C⁰`) is the content of
666	`cechAugmented_exact`. Project-local. -/
667	noncomputable def cechComplexOnX (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
668	    CochainComplex X.Modules ℕ :=
669	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex X.Modules).obj
670	    (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F))
671	
672	/-- **The augmentation point of the {\v C}ech nerve is `F`.** The augmentation
673	object `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left` of the {\v C}ech nerve is `(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F` (the
674	push–pull functor applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`), canonically
675	isomorphic to `F` via the unitors `pushforwardId`/`pullbackId`. Project-local. -/
676	noncomputable def cechNervePointIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
677	    (CechNerve 𝒰 F).left ≅ F :=
678	  (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X)).obj F) ≪≫
679	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X).app F
680	
681	/-- **The augmentation map `ε : F → C⁰` of the {\v C}ech nerve.** Built from the
682	augmentation natural transformation of the augmented cosimplicial object
683	`CechNerve 𝒰 F` (the map `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left ⟶ C⁰`), pre-composed with the unitor
684	`cechNervePointIso` identifying the augmentation point with `F`. This is the map
685	prepended to `cechComplexOnX` to form the augmented {\v C}ech complex. Project-local. -/
686	noncomputable def cechAugmentation (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
687	    F ⟶ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).X 0 :=
688	  (cechNervePointIso 𝒰 F).inv ≫ (CechNerve 𝒰 F).hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0)
689	
690	/-- **Augmentation kills the first alternating-coface differential** (abstract form).
691	For any augmented cosimplicial object `N` in a preadditive category, the augmentation
692	map `N.hom.app [0] : N.left ⟶ (drop N).obj [0]` followed by the degree-0 alternating
693	coface differential `objD (drop N) 0 = δ⁰ - δ¹` is zero. This is the cosimplicial
694	augmentation identity (`ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹`, from naturality of `N.hom` against the two
695	coface maps `[0] ⟶ [1]`); stated abstractly so the proof does not unfold any concrete
696	(heavily whiskered) nerve. Project-local. -/
697	private lemma augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero
698	    {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C] (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented C) :
699	    N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) ≫
700	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
701	        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N) 0 = 0 := by
702	  -- Ascribe the augmentation's codomain to the `𝟭`-free form `N.right.obj ⦋0⦌`; this is
703	  -- the `Comma`'s right functor being `𝟭`, which otherwise pins the composition's middle
704	  -- object to `(𝟭).obj N.right` and blocks every additive distribution lemma's instance.
705	  have hnat : ∀ i : Fin 2,
706	      (N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) : N.left ⟶ N.right.obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) ≫
707	        N.right.δ i = N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 1) := by
708	    intro i
709	    have h := N.hom.naturality (SimplexCategory.δ i)
710	    simpa using h.symm
711	  show (N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) : N.left ⟶ N.right.obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) ≫
712	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD N.right 0 = 0
713	  simp only [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD]
714	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_two]
715	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
716	  -- `erw` (defeq-matching) is needed: the `Comma`'s right functor `𝟭` pins the
717	  -- composition's middle object to `(𝟭).obj N.right`, so `comp_add`/`comp_neg`'s
718	  -- instances only match up to reducible defeq, not syntactically.
719	  erw [Preadditive.comp_add, Preadditive.comp_neg, hnat 0, hnat 1, add_neg_cancel]
720	
721	/-- **The augmentation composes to zero with `d⁰`.** The {\v C}ech augmentation
722	`ε : F → C⁰` followed by the first {\v C}ech differential `d⁰ : C⁰ → C¹` is zero,
723	the cochain-complex condition needed to prepend `ε` to `cechComplexOnX`. It is the
724	cosimplicial augmentation identity: `ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹` (naturality of the
725	augmentation natural transformation against the two coface maps `[0] ⟶ [1]`), so the
726	alternating sum `ε ≫ (δ⁰ - δ¹)` vanishes. Project-local. -/
727	lemma cechAugmentation_comp_d (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
728	    cechAugmentation 𝒰 F ≫ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d 0 1 = 0 := by
729	  rw [cechAugmentation, Category.assoc]
730	  have hd : (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d 0 1 =
731	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
732	        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)) 0 := rfl
733	  rw [hd]
734	  erw [augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero (CechNerve 𝒰 F)]
735	  exact Limits.comp_zero
736	
737	/-- **The augmented {\v C}ech complex on `X`** (Stacks
738	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`). The cochain complex
739	`0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` obtained from the un-augmented {\v C}ech complex `cechComplexOnX`
740	by prepending the augmentation `ε : F → C⁰` (`cechAugmentation`) in degree `0` — so
741	`(cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).X 0 = F` and `(cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).X (p+1) = Cᵖ`. The
742	augmentation condition `ε ≫ d⁰ = 0` is `cechAugmentation_comp_d`. The lemma
743	`cech_augmented_resolution` (`cechAugmented_exact`) asserts this complex is exact —
744	i.e. the {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of `F` in `QCoh(X)`. Project-local. -/
745	noncomputable def cechAugmentedComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
746	    CochainComplex X.Modules ℕ :=
747	  (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).augment (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F) (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F)
748	
749	/-- **The Čech complex computes the higher direct images** (Stacks 02KE;
750	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent` and
751	`lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application`).
752	
753	Let `f : X ⟶ S` be separated and quasi-compact, `F` a quasi-coherent
754	`O_X`-module, and `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X` (so, by separatedness,
755	every intersection is affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative Čech
756	complex compute the higher direct images: for every `i ≥ 0` there is a canonical
757	isomorphism of `O_S`-modules
758	```
759	  Hⁱ(Č•(𝒰, F)) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F.
760	```
761	In particular, over an affine base `S = Spec A`, taking global sections gives
762	`Hⁱ(X, F) = Čⁱ(𝒰, F) = H⁰(S, Rⁱ f_* F)` as `A`-modules.
763	
764	We state the isomorphism as `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` and compare against the
765	derived-functor higher direct image `higherDirectImage` wherever the latter is
766	defined (`HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules`).
767	
768	Route A: reduce to `S` affine; the augmented Čech complex is a termwise
769	`f_*`-acyclic resolution of `F` (each term is a product of pushforwards over
770	affine intersections, acyclic by `CechAcyclic.affine` via the P3b bridge), so
771	the P4 acyclic-resolution lemma (`rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`) gives
772	`Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F`. -/
773	theorem cech_computes_higherDirectImage [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]
774	    (f : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact f] [IsSeparated f] (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
775	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (i : ℕ) :
776	    Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F) := by
777	  -- Route A: reduce to `S` affine; the Čech complex is a termwise acyclic
778	  -- resolution of `F` (by `CechAcyclic.affine`), so `rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`
779	  -- gives `Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F`.
780	  sorry
781	
782	end AlgebraicGeometry
783	
784	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheafification annihilates a locally-zero presheaf
785	
786	These three site-theoretic lemmas are the reusable **Step 2** of the
787	sections/sheafification route to {\v C}ech-resolution exactness
788	(`lem:cech_augmented_resolution`): the homology sheaf of a complex of sheaves of
789	modules is the sheafification of the *presheaf* homology
790	(`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`), and that sheafification is the zero
791	object whenever the presheaf homology is *locally zero* — i.e. it receives a local
792	isomorphism (`J.W`-equivalence) from, or is locally bijective onto, a presheaf that
793	is objectwise zero.
794	
795	They are stated for a general Grothendieck topology and an arbitrary abelian
796	(concrete) target, depend only on Mathlib, and live in this — the most upstream
797	`Cohomology` file — so that the downstream exactness argument (whose section-level
798	vanishing input `sectionCech_affine_vanishing` and the bridge
799	`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify` both reside in files that *import* this one)
Now let me examine the open-immersion and Serre vanishing infrastructure.

892	/-- **Composition formula for higher direct images across an open immersion** (pinned

896	Let `j : U ↪ X` be an open immersion of an affine open `U` into a separated scheme `X`,
897	and `f : X ⟶ S` any morphism. For every quasi-coherent `O_U`-module `H` and every `k ≥ 0`:
898	```
899	  R^k f_*(j_* H) ≅ R^k (f ∘ j)_* H.
900	```
901	Proof route: choose an injective resolution `H → I•` in `U.Modules`; apply `j_*`
902	degreewise to get an `f_*`-acyclic resolution `j_* I•` of `j_* H`
903	(`higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic` for `j_*`-acyclicity, extended to
904	`f_*`-acyclicity via the same Serre-vanishing argument on `U ∩ f⁻¹V`); then
905	`Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` with `G = (pushforward f)` and the identity
906	`(pushforward f) ∘ (pushforward j) = pushforward (j ≫ f)` give the stated isomorphism. -/
907	noncomputable def higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp
908	    [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules] [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]
909	    (j : U ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion j] [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated]
910	    (f : X ⟶ S) (H : U.Modules) (hH : H.IsQuasicoherent) (k : ℕ) :
911	    higherDirectImage f k ((pushforward j).obj H) ≅
912	      higherDirectImage (j ≫ f) k H := by
913	  haveI : IsAffineHom j := isAffineHom_of_affine_separated j
914	  -- Structural plan (all categorical building blocks below are verified to exist):
915	  --   • `G := Scheme.Modules.pushforward f` is additive and preserves finite limits
916	  --     (`inferInstance`), so `Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` applies to it.
917	  --   • The pushed-forward injective resolution `K := (pushforward j).mapHomologicalComplex I•`
918	  --     is a candidate acyclic resolution of `j_* H = (pushforward j).obj H`.
919	  --   • `pushforwardComp j f : pushforward j ⋙ pushforward f ≅ pushforward (j ≫ f)` turns
920	  --     `H^k(G(K))` into `H^k((j ≫ f)_* I•) = R^k (j ≫ f)_* H`.
921	  -- `hacyc`: adjoint route — `pushforward j` preserves injectives via
922	  -- `Injective.injective_of_adjoint (restrictAdjunction j)`, so `j_* Iⁿ` is injective and
923	  -- `(pushforward f)`-acyclic (`IsRightAcyclic.ofInjective`).  Resolution/exactness from
924	  -- `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic`; transport via `pushforwardComp`.
925	  -- Skeleton: apply the acyclic-resolution comparison `rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` to

927	  set K := ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward j).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj

108	    G = `Scheme.Modules.pushforward f`, K = `cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F`, A = F, gives:
109	    `((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F
110	      ≅ ((G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F)).homology i`
111	
112	(d) `pushforward_mapHomologicalComplex_cechComplexOnX` rewrites the right-hand side to
113	    `(CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i`.
114	
115	The final iso `(CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F` is the composite of
116	(d).symm, (c).symm, noting `higherDirectImage f i F = ((pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F`.
117	Wrap in `Nonempty` via `⟨iso⟩`.
118	
119	Additive / PreservesFiniteLimits hypotheses on `pushforward f`: `Additive` is an instance;
120	`PreservesFiniteLimits` is needed for `rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` (via
121	`PreservesFiniteLimits (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)` — left-exact since it is a right adjoint
122	via the global sections adjunction). -/
123	/-- **Čech computes higher direct images — affine-cover form**
124	(blueprint `lem:cech_computes_cohomology_affineCover`; Stacks Tag 02KE).
125	
126	Let `f : X ⟶ S` be a separated quasi-compact morphism with `X` separated, `F` a quasi-coherent
127	`O_X`-module, and `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X`.  Then for every `i ≥ 0` there is an
128	isomorphism between the `i`-th cohomology of the relative Čech complex and the `i`-th higher
129	direct image:
130	```
131	  (CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ R^i f_* F  =  higherDirectImage f i F.
132	```
133	This is the correctly-stated capstone; the frozen `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` in
134	`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` omits `[X.IsSeparated]` and `h𝒰` and is escalated. -/
135	theorem cech_computes_higherDirectImage_of_affineCover [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]
136	    (f : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact f] [IsSeparated f] [X.IsSeparated]
137	    (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (h𝒰 : ∀ i, IsAffine (𝒰.X i))
138	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (i : ℕ) :
139	    Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F) := by
140	  sorry
141	
142	end AlgebraicGeometry

content:
===
`CechSectionIdentification.lean`: the evaluated complex is identified with the concrete
augmented section Čech complex (`cechSection_complex_iso`), which carries the
prepend-`i` contracting homotopy (`cechSection_contractible`, valid since
`V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i`); the in-file glue `isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero`
combines the pair. -/
lemma cechSection_isZero_homology (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules)
    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (i : 𝒰.I₀) (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i) (p : ℕ) :
    let α : X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj
    let cc := ComplexShape.up ℕ
    let K := cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F
    let Kp := ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj K
    let GV :=
      PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)
    IsZero (((GV.mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj Kp).homology p) :=
  isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero (cechSection_complex_iso 𝒰 F V) p
    (cechSection_contractible 𝒰 F V i hiV)

/- Planner strategy:
Route: sections + sheafification (NOT stalks — `SheafOfModules.stalk` is absent from Mathlib).
Step 1: reflect `IsZero (homology p)` through the faithful additive forgetful functor
`SheafOfModules.toSheaf` (it preserves zero morphisms, so it reflects the zero object). Step 2:
the homology SHEAF = sheafification of the PRESHEAF homology, via the project engine
`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify` (HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean). Step 3: the presheaf
homology is `V ↦ Ȟᵖ(V,F)`, locally zero on the affine basis — over each basic affine `D(g) ⊆ Uᵢ`,
`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` gives `F|_{D(g)} ≅ ~M` and
`sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway` (CechAcyclic.lean) kills positive-degree homology;
the basic affines are cofinal (`standard_cover_cofinal` / `affineCoverSystem`), so the map
`0 → presheaf-homology` is locally bijective, hence its sheafification (= the homology sheaf) is
zero. The reusable abelian-sheaf site lemmas `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W` /`_of_W_isZero`/
`_of_isLocallyBijective` (in CechHigherDirectImage.lean, importable) discharge the site-theory half.
The ONE bridge to build here: connect the module-level `homologyIsoSheafify` to those abelian-sheaf
site lemmas via the sheafification square `toSheaf ∘ sheafification ≅ presheafToSheaf ∘ forget`
(cf. `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf`, used in AffineSerreVanishing.lean; see
`analogies/tosheaf-epi.md` and the iter-053 mathlib-analogist report on the toSheaf-reflection
bridge). Diamond-prone — work with `.hom` not `.val`, defeq/`change` not `rw`. Step 4: the
degree-0 augmentation node uses the same spanning-family exactness (`exact_of_isLocalized_span` /
`combDifferential_exact`). Blueprint: `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`.
-/

/-- **The augmented Čech complex is a resolution** (Stacks
`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`; blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`).

Let `X` be a separated scheme, `𝒰` a finite open cover of `X` all of whose members are
affine (so every finite intersection `U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{iₚ}` is affine by separatedness),
and `F` a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module. Then every homology object of the augmented Čech
complex `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F` is zero:
```
  ∀ p, IsZero ((cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).homology p).
```
Equivalently, the {\v C}ech nerve of `𝒰` is a resolution of `F` in `X.Modules`. -/
theorem cechAugmented_exact (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
    (h𝒰 : ∀ i, IsAffine (𝒰.X i)) [X.IsSeparated]
    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
    ∀ p, IsZero ((cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).homology p) := by
  intro p
  -- Step 1: reflect through the faithful, zero-preserving `toSheaf`.
  apply isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms (SheafOfModules.toSheaf X.ringCatSheaf)
  -- Step 2: transport `IsZero` across `homologyIsoSheafify` and the sheafification square.
  set α : X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj with hα
  set cc := ComplexShape.up ℕ with hcc
  set K := cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F with hK
  set P := ((((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj K).homology p) with hP
  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_
    ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso
        (PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify α cc K p) ≪≫
      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf α).app P)
  -- Step 3: the presheaf homology is locally a zero object on the cover-member basis.
  set Q := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj P with hQ
  apply isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Q := Q)
  -- Section-level vanishing on every open `V` contained in a cover member `Uᵢ`:
  -- the restricted cover `{Uₛ ∩ V}` has the member `Uᵢ ∩ V = V`, giving a contracting
  -- (prepend-`i_fix`) homotopy on the augmented section complex over `V`. (F-agnostic.)
  have hSec : ∀ (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (i : 𝒰.I₀),
      V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i → IsZero (Q.obj (Opposite.op V)) := by
    intro V i hiV
    -- Evaluate-at-`V` the underlying presheaf complex: this functor is additive and
    -- preserves homology, so `Q.obj (op V) ≅ Hᵖ(Γ(V, K•))` (the section complex homology).
    set GV := PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V) with hGV
    set Kp := (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj K) with hKp
    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ (GV.mapHomologyIso' cc Kp p).symm
    -- Remaining: the augmented section complex `Γ(V, cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F)` is exact
    -- in degree `p`.  Since `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i`, the restricted cover `{Uₛ ∩ V}` has the
    -- member `coverOpen 𝒰 i ∩ V = V`; prepending that fixed index `i` is a contracting
    -- homotopy `d∘h + h∘d = id` on the section complex (template
    -- `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy` / the objectwise homotopy of FreePresheafComplex),
    -- so every homology object vanishes.  F-agnostic, cover-agnostic.
    -- Step 3(d): discharged by `cechSection_isZero_homology` above (the Sub-brick A chain
    -- of `CechSectionIdentification.lean`, packaged via `isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero`).
    exact cechSection_isZero_homology 𝒰 F V i hiV p
  -- Assemble the covering sieve `{g : V ⟶ U | V ≤ some Uᵢ}` of every open `U`.
  intro U
  refine ⟨{ arrows := fun {V} _ => ∃ i, V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i,
            downward_closed := fun {V W} g hg h => ?_ }, ?_, ?_⟩
  · obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := hg
    exact ⟨i, le_trans (leOfHom h) hi⟩
  · -- membership in the opens topology: cover `U` pointwise via `⨆ᵢ Uᵢ = ⊤`.
    intro x hx
    have hx' : x ∈ (⨆ i, coverOpen 𝒰 i) := by
      have htop : (⨆ i, coverOpen 𝒰 i) = ⊤ := Scheme.OpenCover.iSup_opensRange 𝒰
      rw [htop]; trivial
    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp hx'
    exact ⟨U ⊓ coverOpen 𝒰 i, homOfLE inf_le_left, ⟨i, inf_le_right⟩, hx, hi⟩
  · rintro V g ⟨i, hi⟩
    exact hSec V i hi

end AlgebraicGeometry
Now I have a clear picture. Let me check a few remaining definitions and the build state.

input.description: Read higherDirectImage and coverInterOpen defs, check olean cache

content:
universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

open Scheme.Modules

variable {S X : Scheme.{u}}

/-- The `i`-th higher direct image `Rⁱ f_* F` of a sheaf of modules `F` on `X`
along a morphism `f : X ⟶ S`, defined as the `i`-th right derived functor of the
pushforward functor `f_*` applied to `F`.

For `i = 0` this recovers the ordinary pushforward `R⁰ f_* F = f_* F`.

Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Definition of `Rⁱ f_*`. -/
noncomputable def higherDirectImage [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]
    (f : X ⟶ S) (i : ℕ) (F : X.Modules) : S.Modules :=
  ((pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F

end AlgebraicGeometry
===
noncomputable def freeYoneda : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules :=
  yoneda ⋙ PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj

/-- The open underlying the `i`-th member of an open cover, as `(𝒰.f i).opensRange`. -/
def coverOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (i : 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X := (𝒰.f i).opensRange

/-- The intersection open `⨅ k, U (σ k)` indexed by a tuple `σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀`.

For `κ = Fin (p+1)` this is the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{σ(0)…σ(p)}` indexing the
degree-`p` term of the Čech complex. -/
def coverInterOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X :=
  ⨅ k, coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)

/-- Reindexing along `α : κ' → κ` enlarges the intersection open: precomposing a tuple with
`α` keeps a subset of the indices, so the intersection grows. This `≤` supplies the
representable index-dropping maps that define the Čech differential. -/
lemma coverInterOpen_comp_le (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ κ' : Type} (α : κ' → κ) (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
    coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α) :=
  le_iInf fun k => iInf_le _ (α k)

/-- A coproduct injection transported along an equality of indices: `eqToHom` of the induced

content:
/-
Copyright (c) 2024 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Joël Riou
-/
module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Generators
public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Abelian
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Comma.Over.Pullback

/-!
# Quasicoherent sheaves

A sheaf of modules is quasi-coherent if it admits locally a presentation as the
cokernel of a morphism between coproducts of copies of the sheaf of rings.
When these coproducts are finite, we say that the sheaf is of finite presentation.

## References

* https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01BD

-/

@[expose] public section

universe w u v₁ v₂ u₁ u₂

open CategoryTheory Limits

variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
  {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}

namespace SheafOfModules

section

variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]

/-- A global presentation of a sheaf of modules `M` consists of a family `generators.s`
of sections `s` which generate `M`, and a family of sections which generate
the kernel of the morphism `generators.π : free (generators.I) ⟶ M`. -/
structure Presentation (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) where
  /-- generators -/
  generators : M.GeneratingSections
  /-- relations -/
  relations : (kernel generators.π).GeneratingSections

/-- A global presentation of a sheaf of module if finite if the type
of generators and relations are finite. -/
class Presentation.IsFinite {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (p : M.Presentation) : Prop where
  isFiniteType_generators : p.generators.IsFiniteType := by infer_instance
  isFiniteType_relations : p.relations.IsFiniteType := by infer_instance

attribute [instance] Presentation.IsFinite.isFiniteType_generators
  Presentation.IsFinite.isFiniteType_relations

@[deprecated Presentation.IsFinite.isFiniteType_relations (since := "2026-04-14")]
lemma Presentation.IsFinite.finite_relations {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (p : M.Presentation)
    [p.IsFinite] : Finite p.relations.I := GeneratingSections.IsFiniteType.finite

end

noncomputable section

variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
  [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)] {ι σ : Type u}

/-- Given two morphisms of sheaves of `R`-modules `f : free ι ⟶ free σ` and `g : free σ ⟶ M`
satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0` and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`, we obtain
generators of `Presentation M`. -/
@[simps! I s]
def generatorsOfIsCokernelFree {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) : M.GeneratingSections where
  I := σ
  s := M.freeHomEquiv g
  epi := by simpa using epi_of_isColimit_cofork H'

96	/-- **Stacks 01I8, `lemma-widetilde-pullback`.** Restriction of an `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module to the basic
97	open `D(f)`, transported to a sheaf of `𝒪_{Spec R_f}`-modules along the affine identification
98	`D(f) ≅ Spec R_f`.  Built by restricting first along the open immersion `D(f) ↪ Spec R`, then along
99	the (iso, hence open) inverse of `basicOpenIsoSpecAway f`.  Project-local: assembles two Mathlib
100	restrictions into the single transport used by the affine quasi-coherence equivalence. -/
101	noncomputable def modulesRestrictBasicOpen (F : (Spec R).Modules) :
102	    (Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away f))).Modules :=
103	  (F.restrict (specBasicOpen f).ι).restrict (basicOpenIsoSpecAway f).inv
104	
105	/-- **Stacks 01I8, `lemma-widetilde-pullback`.** The transported restriction
106	`modulesRestrictBasicOpen f F` is canonically isomorphic to the inverse image of `F` along the
107	localisation morphism `Spec R_f ⟶ Spec R`.  This identifies the double restriction with a single
108	pullback, and is the comparison isomorphism `F|_{D(f)} ≅ F_{(f)}` of the blueprint.  Project-local:
109	reconciles the (good-defeq) iterated `restrict` with the conceptual inverse-image description. -/
110	noncomputable def modulesRestrictBasicOpenIso (F : (Spec R).Modules) :
111	    modulesRestrictBasicOpen f F ≅
112	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (specAwayToSpec f)).obj F :=
113	  ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorComp (basicOpenIsoSpecAway f).inv (specBasicOpen f).ι).app F).symm
114	    ≪≫ (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback (specAwayToSpec f)).app F
115	
116	/-- The localisation morphism `Spec R_f ⟶ Spec R` of `specAwayToSpec` is exactly the spectrum of the
117	away-localisation ring map `R → R_f`.  Project-local: identifies the geometric transport map with
118	the algebraic `Spec.map (algebraMap …)`, so that pullback along it computes base change. -/
119	theorem specAwayToSpec_eq :
120	    specAwayToSpec f = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away f))) := by

122	  exact (IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq_hom_fac _ _ _).symm

141	    (by ext : 2; exact (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.1.map_id _)

    (F : C ⥤ D) [F.Additive] (n : ℕ) :
    (F.rightDerived n).obj X ≅

content:
def descCompHomotopy {X Y Z : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (I : InjectiveResolution X)
    (J : InjectiveResolution Y) (K : InjectiveResolution Z) :
    Homotopy (desc (f ≫ g) K I) (desc f J I ≫ desc g K J) := by
  apply descHomotopy (f ≫ g) <;> simp

-- We don't care about the actual definitions of these homotopies.
/-- Any two injective resolutions are homotopy equivalent. -/
def homotopyEquiv {X : C} (I J : InjectiveResolution X) :
    HomotopyEquiv I.cocomplex J.cocomplex where
  hom := desc (𝟙 X) J I
  inv := desc (𝟙 X) I J
  homotopyHomInvId := (descCompHomotopy (𝟙 X) (𝟙 X) I J I).symm.trans <| by
    simpa [id_comp] using descIdHomotopy _ _
  homotopyInvHomId := (descCompHomotopy (𝟙 X) (𝟙 X) J I J).symm.trans <| by
    simpa [id_comp] using descIdHomotopy _ _

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem homotopyEquiv_hom_ι {X : C} (I J : InjectiveResolution X) :
    I.ι ≫ (homotopyEquiv I J).hom = J.ι := by simp [homotopyEquiv]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem homotopyEquiv_inv_ι {X : C} (I J : InjectiveResolution X) :
    J.ι ≫ (homotopyEquiv I J).inv = I.ι := by simp [homotopyEquiv]

end Abelian

end InjectiveResolution

section

variable [Abelian C]

/-- An arbitrarily chosen injective resolution of an object. -/
abbrev injectiveResolution (Z : C) [HasInjectiveResolution Z] : InjectiveResolution Z :=
  (HasInjectiveResolution.out (Z := Z)).some

variable (C)
variable [HasInjectiveResolutions C]

/-- Taking injective resolutions is functorial,
if considered with target the homotopy category
(`ℕ`-indexed cochain complexes and cochain maps up to homotopy).
-/
def injectiveResolutions : C ⥤ HomotopyCategory C (ComplexShape.up ℕ) where
  obj X := (HomotopyCategory.quotient _ _).obj (injectiveResolution X).cocomplex
  map f := (HomotopyCategory.quotient _ _).map (InjectiveResolution.desc f _ _)
  map_id X := by
    rw [← (HomotopyCategory.quotient _ _).map_id]
    apply HomotopyCategory.eq_of_homotopy
    apply InjectiveResolution.descIdHomotopy
  map_comp f g := by
    rw [← (HomotopyCategory.quotient _ _).map_comp]
    apply HomotopyCategory.eq_of_homotopy
    apply InjectiveResolution.descCompHomotopy
variable {C}

/-- If `I : InjectiveResolution X`, then the chosen `(injectiveResolutions C).obj X`
is isomorphic (in the homotopy category) to `I.cocomplex`. -/
def InjectiveResolution.iso {X : C} (I : InjectiveResolution X) :
    (injectiveResolutions C).obj X ≅
      (HomotopyCategory.quotient _ _).obj I.cocomplex :=
  HomotopyCategory.isoOfHomotopyEquiv (homotopyEquiv _ _)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc]
lemma InjectiveResolution.iso_hom_naturality {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y)
24:* `CategoryTheory.InjectiveResolution.descHomotopy`: Any two such descents are homotopic.

103:    (J : InjectiveResolution Z) : J.ι ≫ desc f I J = (CochainComplex.single₀ C).map f ≫ I.ι := by
110:    J.ι.f 0 ≫ (desc f I J).f 0 = f ≫ I.ι.f 0 :=

instance (i : ι) : (homologyFunctor V c i).Additive := by
  have := Functor.additive_of_iso (homologyFunctorFactors V c i).symm
  exact Functor.additive_of_full_essSurj_comp (quotient V c) _

end

end HomotopyCategory

namespace CategoryTheory

variable {V} {W : Type*} [Category* W] [Preadditive W]

/-- An additive functor induces a functor between homotopy categories. -/
@[simps! obj]
def Functor.mapHomotopyCategory (F : V ⥤ W) [F.Additive] (c : ComplexShape ι) :
    HomotopyCategory V c ⥤ HomotopyCategory W c :=
  CategoryTheory.Quotient.lift _ (F.mapHomologicalComplex c ⋙ HomotopyCategory.quotient W c)
    (fun _ _ _ _ ⟨h⟩ => HomotopyCategory.eq_of_homotopy _ _ (F.mapHomotopy h))

@[simp]
lemma Functor.mapHomotopyCategory_map (F : V ⥤ W) [F.Additive] {c : ComplexShape ι}
    {K L : HomologicalComplex V c} (f : K ⟶ L) :
    (F.mapHomotopyCategory c).map ((HomotopyCategory.quotient V c).map f) =
      (HomotopyCategory.quotient W c).map ((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).map f) :=
  rfl

/-- The obvious isomorphism between
`HomotopyCategory.quotient V c ⋙ F.mapHomotopyCategory c` and
`F.mapHomologicalComplex c ⋙ HomotopyCategory.quotient W c` when `F : V ⥤ W` is
an additive functor. -/
def Functor.mapHomotopyCategoryFactors (F : V ⥤ W) [F.Additive] (c : ComplexShape ι) :
    HomotopyCategory.quotient V c ⋙ F.mapHomotopyCategory c ≅
      F.mapHomologicalComplex c ⋙ HomotopyCategory.quotient W c :=
  CategoryTheory.Quotient.lift.isLift _ _ _

403	private lemma coversTop_preimage_of_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) {I : Type*}
404	    (Z : I → X.Opens) (hZ : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).CoversTop Z) :
405	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).CoversTop (fun i => φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Z i) := by
406	  intro W x hx
407	  obtain ⟨U', f, hf, hU'⟩ := hZ ⊤ (φ.inv.base x) (by trivial)
408	  obtain ⟨i, ⟨g⟩⟩ := hf
409	  have hxZ : φ.inv.base x ∈ Z i := (leOfHom g) hU'
410	  refine ⟨W ⊓ (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Z i), homOfLE inf_le_left,
411	    ⟨i, ⟨homOfLE inf_le_right⟩⟩, ⟨hx, ?_⟩⟩
412	  exact hxZ
413	
414	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
415	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
416	-- The heartbeat bumps are required: `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` triggers
417	-- synthesis of the doubly-sliced opens `HasSheafify`/`WEqualsLocallyBijective` instances
418	-- `((J.over X).over Y)…`, whose default-heartbeat search times out (it succeeds when bumped).
419	/-- **Reduction of quasi-coherence of an iso-pushforward to the per-slice case** (Need #1 `hqc`,
420	`of_coversTop` wiring). Given a quasi-coherence datum `q` for `H` and quasi-coherence of the
421	restricted pushforward `(φ_* H).over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ (q.X i))` over each preimage cover member, the
422	pushforward `φ_* H` is quasi-coherent.  Project-local: wires Mathlib's
423	`IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` to the cover-transport `coversTop_preimage_of_iso`, isolating the
424	residual per-slice obligation. -/
425	lemma pushforward_iso_qcoh_of_slice_qcoh {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) (H : X.Modules)
426	    (q : SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.{u, u, u, u} H)
427	    (hslice : ∀ i, (((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H).over
428	        (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i)).IsQuasicoherent) :
429	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H).IsQuasicoherent := by
430	  haveI : ∀ i, (((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H).over
431	      (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i)).IsQuasicoherent := hslice
432	  exact SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop
433	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H) (fun i => φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i)
434	    (coversTop_preimage_of_iso φ q.X q.coversTop)
435	
436	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the slice structure-sheaf ring map `ψ_r` (Need #1 `hqc`)
437	
438	The genuine wall of `hqc`: a single cross-ring slice structure-sheaf ring map `ψ_r` from which the
439	colimit-preserving `SheafOfModules.pullback ψ_r` is built.  For a scheme iso `φ : X ≅ Y` and an open
440	`Uᵢ ⊆ X` with image `Vᵢ = φ.inv⁻¹ᵁ Uᵢ ⊆ Y`, the underlying opens-equivalence functor is
441	`F₀ = Opens.map φ.inv.base : Opens X ⥤ Opens Y` (sending `Uᵢ` to `Vᵢ` by `rfl`).  Its slice version
442	`Over.post F₀ : Over Uᵢ ⥤ Over Vᵢ` is continuous and final (both because `F₀` is an equivalence,
443	`φ` being an iso).  The map `ψ_r` is the slice of the structure-sheaf comparison
444	`φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom : 𝒪_X ⟶ (F₀.sheafPushforwardContinuous _ _ _).obj 𝒪_Y`, obtained simply by
445	applying the over-pullback functor `(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).overPullback RingCat Uᵢ`; the
446	codomain matches the desired sliced pushforward by the Beck–Chevalley identity
447	`Over.post F₀ ⋙ Over.forget Vᵢ = Over.forget Uᵢ ⋙ F₀` (`rfl`). -/
448	
449	section SlicePsi
450	
451	open TopologicalSpace
452	
453	variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) (Ui : X.Opens)
454	
455	/-- `Opens.map φ.inv.base` is an equivalence (its base is the homeomorphism underlying `φ`).
456	Project-local instance feeding the continuity and finality of the slice functor `Over.post`. -/
457	instance opensMapInvBase_isEquivalence : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).IsEquivalence :=
458	  inferInstanceAs (Opens.mapMapIso (Scheme.forgetToTop.mapIso φ).symm).functor.IsEquivalence
459	
460	/-- The slice functor `Over.post (Opens.map φ.inv.base) : Over Uᵢ ⥤ Over Vᵢ` is continuous for the
461	over-Grothendieck-topologies.  Project-local: from `coverPreserving_opens_map` lifted to the slices
462	via `CoverPreserving.overPost`, plus `compatiblePreservingOfFlat` (the slice functor is flat, being
463	an equivalence). -/
464	instance overPost_slice_isContinuous :
465	    (Over.post (X := Ui) (Opens.map φ.inv.base)).IsContinuous
466	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over Ui)
467	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui)) :=
468	  Functor.isContinuous_of_coverPreserving
469	    (compatiblePreservingOfFlat _ _)
470	    (CoverPreserving.overPost Ui (coverPreserving_opens_map φ.inv.base))
471	
472	set_option linter.style.longLine false in
473	/-- **The slice structure-sheaf ring map `ψ_r`** (blueprint `lem:slice_structureSheaf_hom`).  The
474	over-pullback (to the slice `Over Uᵢ`) of the structure-sheaf comparison `φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom`.
475	Its codomain is the sliced pushforward of `𝒪_Y` along `Over.post (Opens.map φ.inv.base)`, identified
476	with the over-pullback of the un-sliced pushforward by the Beck–Chevalley `rfl`-equality
477	`Over.post F ⋙ Over.forget = Over.forget ⋙ F`.  Project-local: the single cross-ring datum from
478	which `SheafOfModules.pullback ψ_r` (Need #1 `hqc`) is built. -/
479	noncomputable def sliceStructureSheafHom : (X.ringCatSheaf.over Ui) ⟶
480	    ((Over.post (X := Ui) (Opens.map φ.inv.base)).sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat
481	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over Ui)
482	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui))).obj
483	        (Y.ringCatSheaf.over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui)) :=
484	  ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).overPullback RingCat Ui).map (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom)
485	
486	/-- The presheaf-of-modules pushforward along `ψ_r.hom` admits a right adjoint (the categories are
487	small).  Project-local instance feeding the sheaf-level right-adjointness. -/
488	instance sliceStructureSheafHom_pre_isRightAdjoint :
489	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ Ui).hom).IsRightAdjoint :=
490	  Functor.isRightAdjoint_of_leftAdjointObjIsDefined_eq_top
491	    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackObjIsDefined_eq_top.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ Ui).hom)
492	
493	set_option maxHeartbeats 4000000 in
494	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
495	-- The heartbeat bumps are required: the derivation triggers synthesis of the sliced-site
496	-- `HasWeakSheafify`/`WEqualsLocallyBijective` instances for `(gt Y).over Vᵢ`, whose
497	-- default-heartbeat search times out (it succeeds when bumped).
498	/-- **`SheafOfModules.pushforward ψ_r` admits a right adjoint** — the obligation that makes the
499	left-adjoint pullback `SheafOfModules.pullback ψ_r` available (blueprint
500	`lem:slice_structureSheaf_hom`, the `IsRightAdjoint` clause).  Project-local instance. -/
501	instance sliceStructureSheafHom_isRightAdjoint :
502	    (SheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ Ui)).IsRightAdjoint :=
503	  inferInstance
504	
505	end SlicePsi
506	
507	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — slice equivalence for `φ : X ≅ Y` and its continuity
508	
509	The slice equivalence `Over Uᵢ ≌ Over Vᵢ` (`Vᵢ = φ.inv⁻¹ᵁ Uᵢ`) induced by a scheme iso `φ`, with
510	BOTH its forward and inverse slice functors shown continuous for the over-Grothendieck-topologies.
511	These are the site-equivalence inputs (continuity instances) required by
512	`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` / `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` to build the
513	two-pushforward slice adjunction (blueprint `lem:pushforward_slice_two_adjunction`, the `hqc` wall).
514	The inverse continuity carries the `Over.post (Opens.map φ.hom.base) ⋙ Over.map (unitIso.inv)`
515	correction; `Over.map` is always continuous (`instIsContinuousOverMapOver`) and the post-factor is
516	cover-preserving via `coverPreserving_opens_map φ.hom.base`. -/
517	section SliceTwoAdj
518	
519	open TopologicalSpace
520	
521	variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) (Ui : X.Opens)
522	
523	/-- `Opens.map φ.hom.base` is an equivalence (twin of `opensMapInvBase_isEquivalence`). -/
524	instance opensMapHomBase_isEquivalence : (Opens.map φ.hom.base).IsEquivalence :=
525	  inferInstanceAs (Opens.mapMapIso (Scheme.forgetToTop.mapIso φ)).functor.IsEquivalence
526	
527	/-- The opens-equivalence `Opens X ≌ Opens Y` induced by the scheme iso `φ`.  Built from
528	`mapMapIso` so that its `functor` is (defeq) `Opens.map φ.inv.base` and its `inverse` is (defeq)
529	`Opens.map φ.hom.base` — both honest `Opens.map`s, with known continuity. -/
530	noncomputable def opensEquivOfIso : TopologicalSpace.Opens X ≌ TopologicalSpace.Opens Y :=
531	  Opens.mapMapIso (Scheme.forgetToTop.mapIso φ).symm

.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean:41:def overEquivalence : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U where
===
-/

open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — continuity of `Opens.overEquivalence`

Mathlib provides `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U` but, as recorded in
its source `## TODO`, does *not* yet prove that the two functors are continuous for the relevant
Grothendieck topologies.  These instances supply exactly that (the gateway brick for the Route B
restrict–over bridge B3): both functors are cover-preserving, and `CompatiblePreserving` is automatic
for the cover-dense functors of an equivalence, so `Functor.IsContinuous` follows. -/

open TopologicalSpace in
/-- The forward functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` preserves covers. -/
theorem Opens.overEquivalence_functor_coverPreserving
    {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X) :
    CoverPreserving ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
      (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor where
  cover_preserve {Y S} hS := by
    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff] at hS
    intro y hy
    obtain ⟨V, f, hVf, hyV⟩ := hS y.1 hy
    obtain ⟨W, h, h', hSh, hfeq⟩ := hVf
    refine ⟨(Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj W,
      (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.map h, ⟨W, h, 𝟙 _, hSh, by simp⟩, ?_⟩
    show y.1 ∈ W.left
    exact leOfHom h' hyV

open TopologicalSpace in
/-- The inverse functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` preserves covers. -/
theorem Opens.overEquivalence_inverse_coverPreserving
    {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X) :
    CoverPreserving (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U)
      (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse where
  cover_preserve {Y S} hS := by
    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
    intro x hx
    obtain ⟨⟨x', hx'U⟩, hx'Y, rfl⟩ := hx
    obtain ⟨P, f, hSf, hxP⟩ := hS ⟨x', hx'U⟩ hx'Y
    exact ⟨((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj P).left,
      ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.map f).left,
      ⟨(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj P, (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.map f, 𝟙 _,
        ⟨P, f, 𝟙 _, hSf, by simp⟩, by simp⟩,
      ⟨⟨x', hx'U⟩, hxP, rfl⟩⟩

open TopologicalSpace in
/-- The forward functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. -/
instance Opens.overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous
    {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X) :
    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsContinuous
      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) :=
  Functor.IsCoverDense.isContinuous _ _ _ (Opens.overEquivalence_functor_coverPreserving U)

open TopologicalSpace in
/-- The inverse functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. -/
instance Opens.overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous
    {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X) :
    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsContinuous
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
  Functor.IsCoverDense.isContinuous _ _ _ (Opens.overEquivalence_inverse_coverPreserving U)

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — restriction of modules to a basic open -/

variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (f : R)

/-- The basic open `D(f) ⊆ Spec R`, as an open subscheme of `Spec R`. -/
abbrev specBasicOpen : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f

/-- The localisation morphism `Spec R_f ⟶ Spec R` factoring through `D(f)`: the inverse of the
affine identification `D(f) ≅ Spec R_f` followed by the open immersion of `D(f)`. -/
noncomputable abbrev specAwayToSpec :
    Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away f)) ⟶ Spec R :=
  (basicOpenIsoSpecAway f).inv ≫ (specBasicOpen f).ι

160	
161	/-- Continuity of `overEquivalence.functor` phrased for the open-subscheme carrier (defeq to the
162	plain subtype, but instance search needs the `toScheme` form to fire). -/
163	instance overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous_toScheme (g : R) :
164	    (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).functor.IsContinuous
165	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(Spec R)).over (specBasicOpen g))
166	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(specBasicOpen g).toScheme) :=
167	  Opens.overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous (specBasicOpen g)
168	
169	/-- Continuity of `overEquivalence.inverse` phrased for the open-subscheme carrier. -/
170	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous_toScheme (g : R) :
171	    (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).inverse.IsContinuous
172	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(specBasicOpen g).toScheme)
173	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(Spec R)).over (specBasicOpen g)) :=
174	  Opens.overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous (specBasicOpen g)
175	
176	/-- The forgetful functor `Over D(g) ⥤ Opens (Spec R)` agrees (via the open-immersion image–preimage
177	identity `ι ''ᵁ (ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V` for `V ≤ D(g)`) with the over-site equivalence followed by the
178	open-immersion `opensFunctor`. In the thin `Opens` category naturality is automatic. Project-local:
179	the geometric datum underlying the Route B restrict–over bridge (B3a). -/
180	noncomputable def overForgetIso (g : R) :
181	    Over.forget (specBasicOpen g) ≅
182	      (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).functor ⋙ (specBasicOpen g).ι.opensFunctor :=
183	  NatIso.ofComponents
184	    (fun V => eqToIso (specBasicOpen_ι_image_overEquivalence_functor g V).symm)
185	    (fun {_ _} _ => Subsingleton.elim _ _)
186	
187	/-- The structure-sheaf comparison `φ` feeding `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`: the over-picture
188	ring sheaf `(Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over D(g)` maps to the `overEquivalence`-pushforward of the
189	subscheme ring sheaf. Built by whiskering `overForgetIso.inv` into `(Spec R).ringCatSheaf`. -/
190	noncomputable def overBasicOpenRingHom (g : R) :
191	    Sheaf.over (Spec R).ringCatSheaf (specBasicOpen g) ⟶
192	      ((Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).functor.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat
193	        ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(Spec R)).over (specBasicOpen g))
194	        (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(specBasicOpen g).toScheme)).obj
195	      (specBasicOpen g).toScheme.ringCatSheaf :=
196	  ⟨Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op (overForgetIso g).inv) (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.val⟩
197	
198	/-- The inverse over-site equivalence followed by `Over.forget` is *definitionally* the
199	open-immersion `opensFunctor` (`overEquivalence.inverse` sends `W` to `⟨Subtype.val '' W, _⟩`, whose
200	`.left` is `ι ''ᵁ W`). Project-local: the (trivial) reverse datum of the B3 bridge. -/
201	noncomputable def overForgetInvIso (g : R) :
202	    (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).inverse ⋙ Over.forget (specBasicOpen g) ≅
203	      (specBasicOpen g).ι.opensFunctor :=
204	  Iso.refl _
205	
206	/-- The reverse structure-sheaf comparison `ψ` feeding `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` (whiskering
207	of `overForgetInvIso.inv`, which is the identity). -/
208	noncomputable def overBasicOpenRingInvHom (g : R) :
209	    (specBasicOpen g).toScheme.ringCatSheaf ⟶
210	      ((Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).inverse.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat
211	        (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(specBasicOpen g).toScheme)
212	        ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(Spec R)).over (specBasicOpen g))).obj
213	      (Sheaf.over (Spec R).ringCatSheaf (specBasicOpen g)) :=
214	  ⟨Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op (overForgetInvIso g).inv) (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.val⟩
215	
216	/-- **Route B, step B3 (the load-bearing bridge — engine).** The equivalence of categories of sheaves
217	of modules between the open subscheme `D(g)` and the over-site `(Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over D(g)`,
218	obtained from `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` along the (continuous) over-site equivalence
219	`Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)` fed the structure-sheaf comparison data
220	`overBasicOpenRingHom`/`overBasicOpenRingInvHom`. Its functor sends a subscheme module
221	`F.restrict ι` to the over-picture restriction `F.over D(g)` (agreeing on sections by `restrict_obj`);
222	this object correspondence is the bridge `overBasicOpenIsoRestrict` consumed by B4. -/
223	noncomputable def modulesOverBasicOpenEquivalence (g : R) :
224	    (specBasicOpen g).toScheme.Modules ≌
225	      SheafOfModules.{u} ((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over (specBasicOpen g)) :=
226	  pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g))
227	    (overBasicOpenRingHom g) (overBasicOpenRingInvHom g)
228	    (by
229	      refine NatTrans.ext (funext fun (V : (Opens ↥(specBasicOpen g))ᵒᵖ) => ?_)
230	      simp only [overBasicOpenRingHom, overBasicOpenRingInvHom, NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app, NatTrans.op_app,
231	        Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.op_obj]
232	      erw [← Functor.map_comp]
233	      exact congrArg (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.val.map (Subsingleton.elim _ _))
234	    (by
235	      refine NatTrans.ext (funext fun (V : (Over (specBasicOpen g))ᵒᵖ) => ?_)
236	      simp only [overBasicOpenRingHom, overBasicOpenRingInvHom, NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app, NatTrans.op_app,
237	        Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.op_obj, NatTrans.id_app,
238	        overForgetInvIso, Iso.refl_inv]
239	      erw [← Functor.map_comp]
240	      exact (congrArg (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.val.map (Subsingleton.elim _ (𝟙 _))).trans
241	        ((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.val.map_id _))
242

244	/-- **Route B, step B3 object iso.** The bridge: the inverse engine applied to the over-picture
245	restriction `M.over D(g)` is the honest subscheme restriction `M.restrict ι`. -/
246	noncomputable def overBasicOpenIsoRestrict (g : R) (M : (Spec R).Modules) :
247	    (modulesOverBasicOpenEquivalence g).inverse.obj (M.over (specBasicOpen g)) ≅
248	      M.restrict (specBasicOpen g).ι := by

251	    (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).inverse (Over.forget (specBasicOpen g))
252	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(specBasicOpen g).toScheme)
253	    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(Spec R)).over (specBasicOpen g))
254	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(Spec R))
255	  refine (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp (G := Over.forget (specBasicOpen g))
256	    (R' := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf)
257	    (overBasicOpenRingInvHom g)
258	    (𝟙 ((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over (specBasicOpen g)))).app M ≪≫ ?_
259	  refine (SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (F := (specBasicOpen g).ι.opensFunctor) ?heq).app M
260	  ext U : 3
261	  simp [overBasicOpenRingInvHom, overForgetInvIso, Scheme.Opens.ι_appIso]
262	  rfl
263	
264	/-- `(basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv` is an isomorphism, so its `Opens.map` is final; hence the
265	unit-comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` of the affine restriction is an isomorphism. -/
266	instance pullbackObjUnitToUnit_isIso_basicOpen (g : R) :
267	    IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit
268	      ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv).toRingCatSheafHom) := by
269	  haveI : IsIso (((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv).base) := inferInstance
270	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv).base).Final := by
271	    haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv).base).IsEquivalence :=
272	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.mapMapIso
273	        (asIso ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv).base)).isEquivalence_functor
274	    infer_instance
275	  infer_instance
276	
277	/-- The restriction-along-the-affine-identification functor sends the structure-sheaf unit to the
278	structure-sheaf unit: `(restrict (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv).obj 𝟙_{D(g)} ≅ 𝟙_{Spec R_g}`. Built
279	from `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an iso since `(basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv`
280	is an isomorphism, hence its `Opens.map` is final). Extracted as its own declaration so the
281	instance search runs in a clean context. -/
282	noncomputable def restrictBasicOpenUnitIso (g : R) :
283	    (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor.{u} (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv).obj
284	        (SheafOfModules.unit (specBasicOpen g).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) ≅
285	      SheafOfModules.unit (Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away g))).ringCatSheaf :=
286	  (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv).app _ ≪≫
287	    @asIso _ _ _ _ _ (pullbackObjUnitToUnit_isIso_basicOpen g)
288	
289	-- The transport of a presentation across the engine equivalence and the affine restriction drives a
290	-- deep instance search (sheafification + colimit-preservation over the localized site); the default

292	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 400000 in

294	/-- **Route B, step B4.** If `M.over U` carries a presentation and `D(g) ⊆ U`, then the affine
295	restriction `modulesRestrictBasicOpen g M`, as a `(Spec R_g).Modules`-object, admits a global
296	presentation. Assembled by transporting the B2 over-presentation across the B3 bridge isomorphism
297	`overBasicOpenIsoRestrict` and the restriction along the affine identification. -/

299	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (U : (Spec R).Opens)
300	    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) (g : R) (hg : specBasicOpen g ≤ U) :
301	    (modulesRestrictBasicOpen g M).Presentation := by
302	  letI P2 : (M.over (specBasicOpen g)).Presentation := presentationOverBasicOpen M U P g hg
303	  letI P3 : ((modulesOverBasicOpenEquivalence g).inverse.obj
304	      (M.over (specBasicOpen g))).Presentation :=
305	    P2.map (modulesOverBasicOpenEquivalence g).inverse (.refl _)
306	  letI P4 : (M.restrict (specBasicOpen g).ι).Presentation :=
307	    Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u} (overBasicOpenIsoRestrict g M).hom P3
308	  -- The instance is supplied as `hpc` (and the functor universe pinned to `u`): inline instance

310	  haveI hpc : Limits.PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u, u, u, u + 1, u + 1}
311	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor.{u} (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv) := inferInstance
312	  exact @SheafOfModules.Presentation.map _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ P4
313	    (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor.{u} (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv) hpc
314	    (restrictBasicOpenUnitIso g)
315	
316	end RestrictOverBridge

attribute [inherit_doc HasFiniteBiproducts] HasFiniteBiproducts.out

variable {J}

theorem hasBiproductsOfShape_of_equiv {K : Type w'} [HasBiproductsOfShape K C] (e : J ≃ K) :
    HasBiproductsOfShape J C :=
  ⟨fun F =>
    let ⟨⟨h⟩⟩ := HasBiproductsOfShape.has_biproduct (F ∘ e.symm)
    let ⟨c, hc⟩ := h
    HasBiproduct.mk <| by
      simpa only [Function.comp_def, e.symm_apply_apply] using
        LimitBicone.mk (c.whisker e) ((c.whiskerIsBilimitIff _).2 hc)⟩

instance (priority := 100) hasBiproductsOfShape_finite [HasFiniteBiproducts C] [Finite J] :
    HasBiproductsOfShape J C := by
  rcases Finite.exists_equiv_fin J with ⟨n, ⟨e⟩⟩
  haveI : HasBiproductsOfShape (Fin n) C := HasFiniteBiproducts.out n
  exact hasBiproductsOfShape_of_equiv C e

instance (priority := 100) hasFiniteProducts_of_hasFiniteBiproducts [HasFiniteBiproducts C] :
    HasFiniteProducts C where
  out _ := ⟨fun _ => hasLimit_of_iso Discrete.natIsoFunctor.symm⟩

/-- **Engine for Stacks 01XJ.** Homology of a complex of sheaves of modules is the
sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of its underlying
presheaf complex:
`K.homology i ≅ (sheafification α).obj ((forget K).homology i)`.

The proof transports homology across the counit isomorphism `sheafify (forget K) ≅ K`
of `counitComplexIso` and then uses that `sheafification α` preserves homology
(it is exact: preserves finite limits and, as a left adjoint, finite colimits),
via `Functor.mapHomologyIso'`.

Project-local: `Mathlib` has the exactness ingredients but not this packaged
"cohomology sheaf = sheafify of objectwise cohomology" comparison. -/
noncomputable def homologyIsoSheafify (K : HomologicalComplex (SheafOfModules.{v} R) cc)
    (i : ιc) :
    K.homology i ≅ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).obj
      ((((SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex
        cc).obj K).homology i) :=
  (HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor _ cc i).mapIso (counitComplexIso α cc K).symm ≪≫
    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).mapHomologyIso' cc
      (((SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex
        cc).obj K) i

end PresheafOfModules

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Rⁿ f_*` as a sheafified presheaf homology -/

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

variable {S X : Scheme.{u}}

/-- The underlying presheaf-of-modules complex of the pushed-forward injective
resolution `f_* I^•`, whose `n`-th objectwise homology is the cohomology presheaf
`V ↦ Hⁿ(f⁻¹(V), G)` (with the implicit choice of injective resolution `I`).

Project-local: the presheaf whose sheafification is `Rⁿ f_* G`. -/
noncomputable def pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex
    [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules] (f : X ⟶ S) {G : X.Modules}
    (I : InjectiveResolution G) :
    HomologicalComplex (PresheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf.obj) (ComplexShape.up ℕ) :=
  ((SheafOfModules.forget S.ringCatSheaf ⋙
      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 S.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapHomologicalComplex
        (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
    (((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj I.cocomplex)

/-- **Stacks 01XJ, resolution form.** For any chosen injective resolution
`I : InjectiveResolution G`, the `n`-th higher direct image `Rⁿ f_* G` is the
sheafification of the presheaf-level homology of the pushed-forward resolution
`f_* I^•`. Concretely, `Rⁿ f_* G ≅ sheafify(V ↦ Hⁿ((f_* I^•)(V)))`, and the
presheaf `V ↦ Hⁿ((f_* I^•)(V)) = Hⁿ(I^•(f⁻¹ V))` is the cohomology presheaf
`V ↦ Hⁿ(f⁻¹(V), G)` of the blueprint statement.

The proof composes `InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj` (computing the right
derived functor `Rⁿ f_* = (f_*).rightDerived n` via the resolution) with the
sheafification engine `PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`.

Project-local: the resolution-dependent form of `lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf`;
the identification of the displayed presheaf with the absolute cohomology presheaf
`V ↦ Hⁿ(f⁻¹(V), G)` is the remaining hand-off step. -/
noncomputable def higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology
    [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules] (f : X ⟶ S) (n : ℕ) {G : X.Modules}
    (I : InjectiveResolution G) :
    higherDirectImage f n G ≅
      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 S.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
        ((pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex f I).homology n) :=
  I.isoRightDerivedObj (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f) n ≪≫
    PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify (𝟙 S.ringCatSheaf.obj) (ComplexShape.up ℕ)
      (((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
        I.cocomplex) n

end AlgebraicGeometry

{R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)]

variable [∀ X, (J.over X).HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)]
  [∀ X, HasSheafify (J.over X) AddCommGrpCat]
  [∀ X, (J.over X).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]

/-- Given a sheaf of `R`-modules `M` and a `Presentation M`, we may construct the quasi-coherent
data on the trivial cover. -/
@[simps]
def Presentation.quasicoherentData {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M) :
    QuasicoherentData M where
  I := C
  X := id
  coversTop x := GrothendieckTopology.covering_of_eq_top J <| by
    rw [Sieve.ext_iff]
    intro _ f
    simpa [Sieve.top_apply, iff_true] using ⟨x, Nonempty.intro f⟩
  presentation x := P.map (pushforward (𝟙 (R.over x))) (by rfl)

/-- If a sheaf of `R`-modules `M` has a presentation, then `M` is quasi-coherent. -/
theorem Presentation.isQuasicoherent {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M) :
    IsQuasicoherent M where
  nonempty_quasicoherentData := Nonempty.intro (Presentation.quasicoherentData P)

/-- Mapping quasicoherent data under an isomorphism. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def QuasicoherentData.ofIsIso {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : M ⟶ N) [IsIso f]
    (σ : M.QuasicoherentData) : N.QuasicoherentData where
  I := σ.I
  X := σ.X
  coversTop := σ.coversTop
  presentation i := Presentation.ofIsIso (f.over (σ.X i)) (σ.presentation i)

instance : (isQuasicoherent R).IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms where
  of_iso e := by
    intro ⟨⟨q⟩⟩
    exact ⟨⟨q.ofIsIso e.hom⟩⟩

instance {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : M ⟶ N) [IsIso f] (σ : M.QuasicoherentData)
    [σ.IsFinitePresentation] : (σ.ofIsIso f).IsFinitePresentation where
  isFinite_presentation i := by
    dsimp
    exact inferInstanceAs ((σ.presentation i).ofIsIso _).IsFinite

instance : (isFinitePresentation R).IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms where
  of_iso e := by
    intro ⟨σ, hσ⟩
    exact ⟨σ.ofIsIso e.hom, inferInstance⟩

===

lemma pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) (H : X.Modules)
    (hH : H.IsQuasicoherent) :
    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H).IsQuasicoherent := by
  obtain ⟨qcd⟩ := hH.nonempty_quasicoherentData
  refine pushforward_iso_qcoh_of_slice_qcoh φ H qcd (fun i => ?_)
  -- Per-slice: build a presentation of `(Φ H).over (φ.inv⁻¹ᵁ qcd.X i)`.
  haveI hFinal : (sliceOversEquiv φ (qcd.X i)).functor.Final := inferInstance
  -- the unit comparison `η : (pullback ψ_r).obj (unit) ≅ unit` (iso since the slice functor is Final).
  have η : (SheafOfModules.pullback.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ (qcd.X i))).obj
        (SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over (qcd.X i))) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit (Y.ringCatSheaf.over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ qcd.X i)) :=
    asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ (qcd.X i)))
  -- transport the local presentation of `H.over Uᵢ` across the colimit-preserving pullback...
  have P1 : ((SheafOfModules.pullback.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ (qcd.X i))).obj
      (H.over (qcd.X i))).Presentation :=
    (qcd.presentation i).map (SheafOfModules.pullback.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ (qcd.X i))) η
  -- ...then across the comparison iso to a presentation of `(Φ H).over Vᵢ`, whence quasi-coherence.
  exact (P1.ofIsIso (pushforwardSlicePullbackIso φ (qcd.X i) H).hom).isQuasicoherent

end SliceTwoAdj

/- Planner strategy:
Part (1): `R^q j_* H` is the sheafification of `V ↦ H^q(j⁻¹V, H)` (presheaf description

/-- **Higher direct images of an affine open immersion vanish** (part (1) of blueprint

/-
